meta.name: TAS-20 Toronto Alexithymia Scale
meta.description: The TAS-20 is a 20-item self-report scale measuring three dimensions of alexithymia: difficulty identifying feelings, difficulty describing feelings, and externally-oriented thinking.
meta.author: Bagby RM, Parker JDA, Taylor GJ (1994)
meta.reference: Bagby RM, Parker JDA, Taylor GJ. The twenty-item Toronto Alexithymia Scale: I. Item selection and cross-validation of the factor structure. J Psychosom Res. 1994;38(1):23-32.
meta.instruction: Please indicate how much you agree or disagree with each of the following statements based on how you generally feel.

questions.text: I am often confused about what emotion I am feeling.
It is difficult for me to find the right words for my feelings.
I have physical sensations that even doctors don't understand.
I am able to describe my feelings easily.
I prefer to analyze problems rather than just describe them.
When I am upset, I don't know if I am sad, frightened, or angry.
I am often puzzled by sensations in my body.
I prefer to just let things happen rather than to understand why they turned out that way.
I have feelings that I can't quite identify.
Being in touch with emotions is essential.
I find it hard to describe how I feel about people.
People tell me to describe my feelings more.
I don't know what's going on inside me.
I often don't know why I am angry.
I prefer talking to people about their daily activities rather than their feelings.
I prefer watching light entertainment shows rather than psychological dramas.
It is difficult for me to reveal my innermost feelings, even to close friends.
I can feel close to someone, even in moments of silence.
I find examination of my feelings useful in solving personal problems.
Looking for hidden meanings in movies or plays distracts from their enjoyment.

ranges.label: No Alexithymia
ranges.description: Your ability to identify and express emotions is within the normal range. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining good emotional awareness
Sharing feelings with others supports mental health
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities

ranges.label: Possible Alexithymia
ranges.description: You may have some degree of alexithymia. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Practice identifying and naming your emotions
Keeping an emotion journal may be helpful
Consider emotional awareness training
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques

ranges.label: Alexithymia
ranges.description: Your score suggests alexithymia, with significant difficulty in identifying and expressing emotions. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Psychotherapy can help improve emotional awareness
Body sensation awareness practices (e.g., mindfulness) may be beneficial
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques

difficulty_identifying.label: Normal
difficulty_identifying.description: Your ability to identify emotions is normal. You can recognize and distinguish your feelings well. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

difficulty_identifying.label: Moderate Difficulty
difficulty_identifying.description: You have some difficulty identifying emotions, sometimes struggling to distinguish different emotional experiences. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

difficulty_identifying.label: Significant Difficulty
difficulty_identifying.description: You have significant difficulty identifying emotions. Professional training to improve emotional awareness is recommended. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

difficulty_describing.label: Normal
difficulty_describing.description: Your ability to describe emotions is normal. You can express your feelings in words well. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

difficulty_describing.label: Moderate Difficulty
difficulty_describing.description: You have some difficulty describing emotions, sometimes struggling to find the right words for your feelings. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

difficulty_describing.label: Significant Difficulty
difficulty_describing.description: You have significant difficulty describing emotions. Practicing with an emotion journal is recommended. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

externally_oriented.label: Normal
externally_oriented.description: Your thinking style is introspective, with good attention to inner experiences. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

externally_oriented.label: Moderately Externally Oriented
externally_oriented.description: Your thinking tends toward external matters, with less attention to inner emotional experiences. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

externally_oriented.label: Highly Externally Oriented
externally_oriented.description: Your thinking is strongly oriented toward external matters. Mindfulness and reflective practices are recommended. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support
